lines.en_lit: bread, they asked for appropriately. was given to them appropriately, From here, the Torah teaches etiquette, that to eat meat only at night, But didn’t Abaye say someone who has a meal should eat it only in the day? We mean to say: Like day. Rabbi Aḥa bar Ya’akov said: At the beginning, the Jewish people were like chickens pecking at the garbage; until Moses came and set specific times to eat,
“While the meat was yet between their teeth, an entire month How these The average people died immediately, the wicked continued to suffer for a month

“And they spread them [vayishteḥu] Reish Lakish said: Do not read it vayishteḥu. Rather, vayishḥatu. teaches that enemies of the Jewish people, were liable to slaughter “Spread out [shato’aḥ]” taught in the name of Rabbi Yehoshua ben Korḥa: Do not read it shato’aḥ but shaḥot. teaches that fell for the Jewish people with the manna. that requires ritual slaughtering [sheḥita], Rabbi said: And do you learn from here? Isn’t it already stated “And he rained meat upon them like dust, and winged birds like the sand of the seas”

And it was taught Rabbi says: “Then you shall slaughter as I have commanded you” This teaches that Moses was commanded in gullet and the windpipe And with a bird the majority one and with an animal the majority of Rather, what the verse states: Shatoaḥ? teaches that fell in layers [mashtiḥin]
it is written “bread” and it is written “oil” and it is written “honey” Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Ḥanina, said: For the youth bread, for the elderly oil, for the children honey.
is written shlav, but we read slav, Rabbi Ḥanina said: The righteous eat it in peace [shalva], the wicked eat it, and it seems to them like thorns [silvin],

Rav Ḥanan bar Rava said: There are four types of quail and these are they: Sikhli, and kivli, and pasyoni, and slav. The best of all sikhli. The worst of all slav. It was as a sparrow, and they would place it in the oven and it expanded until it filled the oven. They would place it upon thirteen loaves last could be eaten only mixed with other food,

Rav Yehuda found among his barrels of wine, Rav Ḥisda found among logs of wood Every day Rava’s sharecropper brought him One day, he did not bring said What is this, He went up to the roof He heard a child say “When I heard, my innards trembled, said: Learn from this that Rav Ḥisda has died. account of the teacher the student eats.
it is written: “And when the layer of dew lifted, And it is written: And when the dew fell Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Ḥanina, said: dew above and dew below, and appeared as if placed in a box [kufsa]

“a fine flaky [meḥuspas] Reish Lakish said: a substance that dissolved [ ] on the palm [ ] of the hand. Rabbi Yoḥanan said: It was a substance that was absorbed in 248 limbs, meḥuspas, is more, Rabbi Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: Meḥuspas is written

The Sages taught: Man did eat the bread of the mighty [abirim]” bread that the ministering angels eat; statement of Rabbi Akiva. When these words were said before Rabbi Yishmael, he said to them go and tell Akiva: Akiva you have erred. Do the ministering angels eat bread? It is already stated “Bread I did not eat and water I did not drink” Rather, how do I establish abirim? bread that was absorbed into 248 limbs [eivarim],
But how do I establish “And you shall have a spade among your weapons, where you can relieve yourself items that the gentile merchants sold them.

Rabbi Elazar ben Perata says: The manna caused even items that the gentile merchants sold them to be digested, But how do I establish “And you shall have a spade among your weapons”? After they sinned, The Holy One, Blessed be He, said: I said they would be like ministering angels now I will trouble them to walk three parasangs

As it is written: “And they camped by the Jordan from Beth-Jeshimoth to Abel-shittim” and Rabba bar bar Ḥana said: I saw that site and it was three parasangs And taught: When relieved themselves they did not relieve ahead of themselves, nor to the side but behind
“But now our soul is dry, there is nothing at all; They said: This manna will eventually swell in our stomachs is there born of a woman who ingests but does not expel

When these words were said before Rabbi Yishmael, he said to them: Do not read abirim. Rather, eivarim, something that was absorbed by 248 limbs. But, how do I establish “And you shall have a spade among your weapons”? From the items that came to them from overseas lands.
Alternatively, “Man [ish] did eat the bread of the mighty”
